title: Circular on Submission of Applications & Regulatory Reporting via Supervisory Intelligence System (SIS) Platform
text:
LABUAN FINANCIAL SERVICES AUTHORITY
(LEMBAGA PERKHIDMATAN KEWANGAN LABUAN)
Circular No.: 322/2025/ALL
Date: 6 June 2025
Association of Labuan Banks (ALB)
Labuan Investment Banks Association (LIBA)
Labuan International Insurance Association (LIIA)
Association of Labuan Trust Companies (ALTC)
Labuan Compliance Officers’ Networking Group
- Labuan Banks Compliance Officers’ Networking Group (LB-CONG)
- LIIA Compliance Officers’ Networking Group (LIIA-CONG)
- ALTC Compliance Officers’ Networking Group (ALTC-CONG)
- Digital Financial Services Compliance Officers’ Networking Group (DFS-CONG)
Dear Sir/Madam,
SUBMISSION OF REGULATORY APPLICATIONS AND REPORTS VIA
SUPERVISORY INTELLIGENCE SYSTEM (SIS) PLATFORM
As part of our ongoing efforts to strengthen data governance and enhance regulatory
reporting efficiency, we are pleased to announce the launch of the Supervisory
Intelligence System (SIS) platform. The platform provides a standardised and
structured process in managing and monitoring the submissions of regulatory
applications and reports to the Supervision Department. This will take effect from 16
June 2025.
2. This Circular is issued to notify Labuan licensed entities and registered
businesses of the new submission requirements. In this regard, we seek the
assistance of:
• Labuan industry associations to disseminate this Circular to the respective
members; and
• Labuan trust companies and managed trust companies to circulate this
Circular to the respective clients, including managed entities such as Labuan
foundations, to ensure full coverage and compliance.
The list of sectors and entities covered under the SIS submission requirements is
provided in Appendix I.
Level 17, Main Office Tower, Financial Park Complex, Jalan Merdeka, 87000 Wilayah Persekutuan Labuan, Malaysia
Tel (+03) 8873 2000 Fax (+03) 8873 2209

Submission of Regulatory Applications and Reports via SIS Platform
Page 2 of 4
3. Submissions via SIS:
i. Regulatory Applications to Supervision Department
The following regulatory applications are to be submitted through the SIS
platform:
a) Appointment of external auditor
b) External service arrangements (ESA) including third party appointed
actuary
c) Extension of time for regulatory submission
ii. Submission of Regulatory Reports
The following regulatory reports are to be submitted via the SIS platform:
a) Audited financial statement
b) Actuarial valuation report
c) Internal audit report
d) Independent audit report on anti-money laundering and counter
financing of terrorism
e) Letter of compliance
f) Annual compliance report
g) Summary of reinsurance arrangements
h) Annual identification and verification for e-KYC
i) Annual summary report of exposure to customers and beneficial
owners from high-risk countries
j) Shariah compliance report
k) Shariah internal audit report
Please ensure documents submitted are complete, accurate, legible,
properly formatted and appropriately named to reflect the content and
reporting period. File names should clearly indicate the type of report and
relevant reporting period (Examples: Audited financial statements FYE 31
Dec 2025, Internal audit report 2025).
With the issuance of this Circular, effective from 16 June 2025, the use of
the following submission platforms is discontinued:
a) COR@L platform - Submission of Audited Financial Statement by
Labuan Entities through COR@L (Circular dated 12 November 2020)
b) Cloud-based submission - Submission of Compliance Report and
Anti-Money Laundering Counter Financing of Terrorism (AML/CFT)
Independent Audit Report to Labuan FSA (Circular dated 8 July 2022)
c) Microsoft OneDrive - Submission of Audited Financial Statements
through Microsoft OneDrive (Circular dated 15 June 2023)

Submission of Regulatory Applications and Reports via SIS Platform
Page 3 of 4
iii. Notification on Appointment/Resignation of CO/DCO
Effective from 16 June 2025, the SIS platform replaces the previous
notification process for the appointment and resignation of Compliance
Officer (CO) and Designated Compliance Officer (DCO), that was
previously carried out via hardcopy or email submission.
4. Please refer to the attached User Guide for instructions on:
• Creating an account
• Accessing the SIS platform
• Submitting applications
• Uploading regulatory documents
• Notifying appointment/resignation of CO/DCO
Only files in PDF, DOCX, JPG, JPEG or PNG format, with a maximum size of 20MB
per file, will be accepted. Submissions outside these parameters may be rejected by
the system.
5. Should you require clarification, please contact the following officers:
Name Contact No. Email Address
Ms. Hafizah Suhaimi 03-8873 2047 hafizah@labuanfsa.gov.my
Mr. Pooi Jun Jin 03-8873 2045 pooijj@labuanfsa.gov.my
Mr. Amirul Firdaus Jenggi 03-2780 3323 amirul@labuanfsa.gov.my
Mr. Sharvin Purushotthaman 03-8873 2139 sharvin@labuanfsa.gov.my
Yours sincerely
For and on behalf of Labuan FSA
MUHAMMAD HANAFIS BORHAN
Director
Supervision Department

Submission of Regulatory Applications and Reports via SIS Platform
Page 4 of 4
APPENDIX I
The submissions of regulatory applications and regulatory reports via SIS platform are
applicable to the following sectors:
1. Labuan banks and investment banks
2. Labuan Islamic banks and Islamic investment banks
3. Labuan insurers and reinsurers
4. Labuan takaful and retakaful operators
5. Labuan captive insurance business and captive takaful business
6. Labuan insurance-related companies (insurance intermediaries) and takaful-
related companies (takaful intermediaries)
7. Labuan trust companies, managed trust companies and private trust companies
8. Labuan money brokers and Islamic money brokers
9. Labuan fund managers, Islamic fund managers and fund administrators
10. Labuan securities licensees and Islamic securities licensees
11. Labuan exchanges
12. Labuan credit token business
13. Labuan leasing companies
14. Labuan international commodity trading companies
15. Labuan factoring business
16. Labuan charitable foundation
17. Labuan company management
18. Labuan payment system
6 June 2025
